data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T035018-3aae5cee/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T035018-3aae5cee/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T035018-3aae5cee/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T035018-3aae5cee/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T035018-3aae5cee/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-3e157810">
    <title>feat(deps): integrate easel/fizeau replacement for DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  Fizeau (the agent-routing service DDx embeds for harness execution
  and routing) was moved from github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau to a
  new home at easel/fizeau, with a new published release. DDx today
  imports github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.15 (cli/go.mod:7)
  across 53 .go files in cli/. The integration is mechanical (import
  path swap + go.mod update + go.sum refresh + verify build/tests).

  Top-priority because: (a) the old import path may be deprecated or
  receive no further releases; (b) we want the latest fizeau release
  features/fixes (likely includes routing or wedge-relevant
  improvements); (c) this is a blocking integration — every other
  agent-side fix depends on stable fizeau access.

  Per user memory feedback_no_local_replace.md: ddx must always import
  a properly tagged release; no go.mod replace directives, even
  temporarily.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/go.mod:7 has `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.15`.
  - 53 .go files in cli/ import github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau or
    a sub-package thereof. Verify with:
        grep -rn "DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau" cli/ --include='*.go' | wc -l
  - The new module path is at easel/fizeau (likely
    github.com/easel/fizeau — confirm via `go list -m
    github.com/easel/fizeau@latest` or by checking the upstream repo
    directly). The latest published release tag should be discovered
    at integration time, not assumed from this bead body.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Discover the new module path and latest tag:
        - Try: `go list -m github.com/easel/fizeau@latest`
        - If that fails, check upstream README / GitHub for the
          canonical Go import path.
        - Confirm a Go-module-friendly tag (e.g., v0.11.x or higher).

  2. Update cli/go.mod:
        - Remove `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.15`.
        - Add `<new-module-path> <new-version>`.
        - Run `go mod tidy` to refresh go.sum.
        - DO NOT add a go.mod replace directive — must import the
          properly tagged release directly.

  3. Sweep the 53 .go files:
        - Replace `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau` →
          `<new-module-path>` in every import statement.
        - Verify no stale references remain:
            grep -rn "DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau" cli/ → must return zero.

  4. Verify the build:
        - cd cli && go build ./... must succeed.
        - cd cli && go test ./... must pass (or at minimum match the
          pre-change baseline; document any breaking changes from the
          new release in the commit message).

  5. If the new release introduces API changes (e.g., renamed types,
     changed function signatures, new required fields):
        - Adapt the call sites minimally to satisfy the new API.
        - Document each adaptation in the commit message.
        - If the API churn is large enough to merit a separate bead,
          file it and note in this bead's commit.

NON-SCOPE
  - Adopting any new fizeau features beyond what's needed to compile
    against the new release.
  - Refactoring DDx's fizeau integration patterns (out of scope; do
    only what's needed to compile and pass existing tests).
  - Changes to ddx-server-side fizeau usage (covered if any; flag if
    out of repo).

INTERSECTIONS
  - All other agent-side beads (ddx-1f774edf, ddx-1c3cad6f, etc.)
    depend on a working fizeau integration. Land this first.
  - User memory feedback_no_local_replace.md applies: no replace
    directives, even as a stepping stone.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/go.mod no longer references github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau; instead references the new easel/fizeau module path at the latest published tag.
2. cli/go.sum is refreshed via `go mod tidy`.
3. grep -rn "DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau" cli/ --include='*.go' returns zero matches.
4. cd cli && go build ./... succeeds.
5. cd cli && go test ./... green (or matches pre-change baseline; document any deltas in the commit message).
6. cli/go.mod contains no `replace github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau =>` or `replace github.com/easel/fizeau =>` directives.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, area:deps, kind:refactor, upstream-fizeau, prevention</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="d1d827ceeca770fe8ed4fe8ca050842639f90835" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T035018-3aae5cee"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>
